# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Pinned obfuscation seed for the v1.1.0 fixture build.
#
# Crafted to mimic real-world minor-version rotation:
#
#   * MOST classes rotate their 4-letter obf name        (aaaa → aaab, etc.)
#   * SOME classes keep the SAME name across the rotation (rare but real)
#   * METHOD letters within a class are MOSTLY STABLE      (the project
#       README §0.2 finding: methods rotate slower than classes)
#   * A FEW method letters do rotate, simulating R8's
#       optimization-driven re-ordering
#   * FIELD letters mostly stay (R8 packs fields by descriptor type and
#       order, which is unchanged between identical source builds)
#
# The on-disk diff between this file and v1.0.0.applymapping.txt is the
# whole point of the fixture: it's the rotation pattern rosetta-frida
# exists to abstract over.
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# ── AIDL-anchored classes (stay at real names; -keep in proguard-rules) ──

# ── RemoteService — the class rename is a NO-OP (methods STABLE) ─────
# This `-> aaab` class pin is dead: RemoteService is the manifest
# `<service>` entry, so R8 keeps the real class name across BOTH builds
# (no v1.0.0→v1.1.0 rotation for this class). The member pins still
# apply. See README's rotation matrix.
com.example.testapp.RemoteService -> aaab:
    java.lang.String ROSETTA_ANCHOR -> a
    int requestCounter -> b
    java.lang.String lastTag -> c
    com.example.testapp.IRemoteService$Stub binder -> d
    void safeError(com.example.testapp.IServiceCallback,int,java.lang.String) -> a

# ── Anonymous Runnable: `bbbb` → `bbbc` ───────────────────────────────
com.example.testapp.RemoteService$1 -> bbbc:
    com.example.testapp.RemoteService this$0 -> a
    android.os.Bundle val$params -> b
    com.example.testapp.IServiceCallback val$callback -> c
    void run() -> run

# ── BlobCache — class STAYS at `cccc` (uncommon but real) ──────────────
# Inside, methods STAY at c/d/e/f/g — illustrating the "method letters
# are noticeably more stable than class names" observation from §0.2.
com.example.testapp.BlobCache -> cccc:
    java.lang.String ROSETTA_ANCHOR -> a
    int MAX_SIZE -> b
    int instanceCount -> c
    com.example.testapp.BlobCache singleton -> d
    java.util.HashMap buffer -> e
    java.lang.String lastEvictedKey -> f
    com.example.testapp.BlobCache getInstance() -> a
    java.lang.Object get(java.lang.String) -> c
    void put(java.lang.String,java.lang.Object) -> d
    void put(java.lang.String,java.lang.Object,long) -> e
    void evict(java.lang.String) -> f
    void clear() -> g

# ── Config — `dddd` → `ddde` (class rotates; FIELDS rotate too) ──────
# Fields shuffle to a/b/d/c/e instead of a/b/c/d/e — simulating R8's
# field reordering pass affecting only this class.
com.example.testapp.Config -> ddde:
    java.lang.String ROSETTA_ANCHOR -> a
    boolean ENABLE_TRACING -> b
    int MAX_RETRIES -> d
    long TIMEOUT_MILLIS -> c
    java.lang.String ENDPOINT_URL -> e
    int currentDebugLevel -> f

# ── Ticket — `eeee` → `eeef` (class rotates; methods STABLE) ──────────
com.example.testapp.Ticket -> eeef:
    java.lang.String value -> a
    long expiryMillis -> b
    java.lang.String ROSETTA_ANCHOR -> c
    com.example.testapp.Ticket fromBytes(byte[]) -> a
    java.lang.String getValue() -> b
    long getExpiry() -> c

# ── Ticket$Companion — `ffff` → `fffg` ─────────────────────────────────
com.example.testapp.Ticket$Companion -> fffg:
    java.lang.String ROSETTA_ANCHOR -> a
    com.example.testapp.Ticket create(java.lang.String) -> a

# ── Ticket$Reader — `gggg` → `gggh` ────────────────────────────────────
com.example.testapp.Ticket$Reader -> gggh:
    com.example.testapp.Ticket this$0 -> a
    java.lang.String ROSETTA_ANCHOR -> b
    java.lang.String readValue() -> a
    long readExpiry() -> b

# ── ErrorCode — `hhhh` → `hhhi` (enum class rotates; methods STABLE) ─
com.example.testapp.ErrorCode -> hhhi:
    com.example.testapp.ErrorCode SUCCESS -> a
    com.example.testapp.ErrorCode TIMEOUT -> b
    com.example.testapp.ErrorCode AUTH_FAILED -> c
    int code -> d
    com.example.testapp.ErrorCode[] $VALUES -> e
    java.lang.String ROSETTA_ANCHOR -> f
    int getCode() -> a

# ── AbstractServiceClient — `iiii` → `iiij` ───────────────────────────
com.example.testapp.AbstractServiceClient -> iiij:
    int INSTANCE_COUNT -> a
    android.content.Context context -> b
    java.lang.String ROSETTA_ANCHOR -> f
    java.lang.String anchorSink -> g
    void connect() -> a
    void disconnect() -> b
    java.lang.Runnable retryHandle() -> c

# ── AbstractServiceClient$1 — `jjjj` → `jjjk` ─────────────────────────
com.example.testapp.AbstractServiceClient$1 -> jjjk:
    com.example.testapp.AbstractServiceClient this$0 -> a
    void run() -> run

# ── RemoteServiceClient — `kkkk` → `kkkl` (class rotates; METHOD `apply` rotates too) ──
# Method `apply` was `e` in v1.0.0 — it moves to `f` here, simulating
# R8 reshuffling the method-name slot after `connect`/`requestTicket`.
com.example.testapp.RemoteServiceClient -> kkkl:
    java.lang.String sessionId -> c
    int flags -> d
    java.lang.String ROSETTA_ANCHOR -> h
    void connect() -> a
    com.example.testapp.Ticket requestTicket(android.os.Bundle) -> c
    java.lang.Object apply(java.lang.Object) -> f

# ── PromiseCallback interface — `llll` → `lllm` ───────────────────────
com.example.testapp.PromiseCallback -> lllm:
    java.lang.String PROMISE_ANCHOR -> e
    java.lang.Object apply(java.lang.Object) -> a
